description: Pre-production architecture hardening. A senior-architect critical review found Strata mostly production-ready (s3-tests 92.7%, QA GO) but with one load-bearing claim the code does not back - per-bucket object sharding + online reshard are advertised as the Cassandra-side answer to RGW's bucket-index ceiling, yet the Cassandra hot path ignores bucket.ShardCount (uses the process-global s.defaultShard at all 9 shardOf call sites) and internal/reshard/runJob is a skeleton that counts rows but never moves them. This cycle makes the claim true: real per-bucket sharding on Cassandra, a genuine online reshard that rewrites rows with a target-only-write + union-read + cleanup-before-flip transitional model, and TiKV+Cassandra parity (Cassandra contract back on a green required CI check). It also closes three accepted QA-cycle P2 residual risks: DeleteObjects direct test (R1), object-access policy-UNION-ACL semantics (R6), and a per-chunk CRC32C verified on the read path to fail loud on plaintext byte-flip (R9). Every behavioural fix ships with red->green proof. Resolved decisions: CRC32C on-by-default (STRATA_CHUNK_CRC_VERIFY); TiKV/memory reshard = immediate-complete no-op job (API success, UI disabled); target-only writes + union-read + post-flip cleanup; reshard console UI in-cycle; Cassandra contract via dedicated nightly job this cycle with per-PR-runner starvation root-cause as a tracked must-fix. Backends in scope: TiKV + memory (canonical per-PR CI), Cassandra (integration, dedicated job), RADOS/ceph + MinIO under integration tags. CI on Linux is the source of truth; many failure modes do not reproduce on a local macOS/lima box. If a story cannot land in one Ralph iteration, split it into US-00N + a trailing story and record the split in progress.txt. Source PRD at tasks/prd-architecture-hardening.md.

userStories.title: Cassandra hot path resolves per-bucket shard count
userStories.description: As an operator who created a bucket with a non-default STRATA_BUCKET_SHARDS, I want reads and writes to that bucket to use its shard count, so the per-bucket sharding the docs promise actually takes effect.
acceptanceCriteria: ALL nine shardOf(key, s.defaultShard) call sites in internal/meta/cassandra/store.go resolve the bucket's ShardCount instead of the global: PutObject:463, GetObject:587, DeleteObject:752, SetObjectReplicationStatus:1887, resolveVersionID:2408 (CRITICAL - resolves ?versionId; missing it 404s every versioned point-read on a non-default bucket), UpdateObjectSSEWrap:4118, and the two further object-mutation sites at :4167 and :4198

The three list/scan sites shardCount := s.defaultShard (ListObjects:820, ListObjectVersions:919) and the helpers bucketIsEmpty:357 / loop :1042 / SampleBucketShardStats:1142 likewise resolve the bucket's ShardCount
A grep assertion in review confirms no remaining s.defaultShard on any object-addressing path (missing any one site reintroduces the 404 bug)

A shard-count resolver cache keyed on bucketID with a short TTL avoids a buckets round-trip on every object op; cache miss falls back to a read; the cache carries BOTH ShardCount (active) and TargetShardCount so in-flight writers route without a second lookup; CompleteReshard invalidates the entry synchronously

Steady-state only (no in-flight reshard transition in this story): a bucket created with shard_count=128 round-trips PUT->GET->LIST->DELETE correctly; a bucket with shard_count=16 likewise; both differ from the process default
TiKV + memory backends unchanged (TiKV is range-scan, shard-agnostic)
New contract case in internal/meta/storetest proves non-default per-bucket shard count round-trips (memory always-on; Cassandra leg under -tags integration)
make vet passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: All 12 object-addressing sites in cassandra/store.go now resolve the bucket's ShardCount via bucketShardCounts (short-TTL cache, carries active+target, invalidated synchronously by StartReshard/CompleteReshard). shardForKey helper replaces shardOf(key,s.defaultShard) at the 9 mutation/point-read sites; the 3 list/scan sites + AllObjectVersions + SampleBucketShardStats fallback + bucketIsEmpty resolve active count. grep confirms no s.defaultShard on object-addressing paths (remaining uses: comments, CreateBucket INSERT, legacy<=0 guards). Generic contract case caseNonDefaultShardRoundTrip (memory+tikv+cassandra). Discriminating red/green proof: integration TestCassandraPerBucketShardResolution — two stores over same keyspace, writer default=128/16, reader default=64, reader must resolve stored count or 404. make vet + memory suite green locally; cassandra+tikv legs under integration/containers (CI). Verified locally CGO_ENABLED=0 on darwin (cgo blocked per progress patterns).

userStories.title: Reshard transitional read/write model (meta contract)
userStories.description: As the system, while a reshard job is in flight I must serve a stable key set - every key visible before the job started stays visible throughout - so clients see no gaps or duplicates during a resize.
acceptanceCriteria: Transitional contract defined: while shard_count_target != 0, writes land in the target layout (fnv%target) and reads return the deduplicated union of source (fnv%active) and target partitions, target row wins on (key, version_id) collision
GetObject point lookups during a job probe both the source-shard and target-shard partition for the key and return the newest by version
ListObjects/ListObjectVersions during a job merge source-union-target with dedup so a half-migrated key is never double-emitted and never missing
A storetest contract case seeds a bucket, flips it into the in-flight state with rows split across both layouts, and asserts the listed/point-read key set equals the pre-flip set exactly
Power-of-two doubling invariant documented in code: target = 2*active, each old shard s maps to s or s+active; no three-way splits (meta.IsValidShardCount enforces power-of-two)
Memory backend (shard-agnostic flat map) returns the same key set - transitional model is a no-op but the contract test still passes
make vet passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Cassandra transitional read/write: target-layout writes, union source+target reads (target wins collision), clear-both-shards overwrite/delete, widened LIST fan-out + cross-shard delete-marker suppression. Memory/TiKV no-op. Proof: caseReshardTransitionalKeySet + integration TestCassandraReshardTransitional + TestCassandraReshardDeleteMarkerNoResurrect.

userStories.title: Reshard worker actually rewrites rows + cleanup
userStories.description: As an operator resizing a hot bucket's shards, I want the reshard worker to physically move each row to its new partition and then flip the active shard count, so post-flip point-GET addresses the right partition.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/reshard/reshard.go::runJob is rewritten: for each source partition scan rows; for every row whose fnv%target != fnv%active, copy it to the target partition (INSERT under the new layout); persist the LastKey watermark per batch for crash-resume

Ordering is cleanup-BEFORE-flip: while the job is in-flight (target != 0) union-read is active, so the worker first deletes each moved source-partition orphan, THEN CompleteReshard LWT-flips active = target and clears shard_count_target - flipping first would let post-flip listing double-emit the moved key
Cleanup is idempotent and crash-safe (re-running after a partial cleanup converges; no row deleted that the target does not already hold)

The flip itself is LWT (IF EXISTS) per the codebase LWT-on-LWT rule, or a quorum read can observe a stale shard_count; do not overclaim atomicity - move+flip is NOT one atomic op, only the flip is atomic

Red/green proof: seed a bucket with N keys at shard_count=64, run a 64->128 reshard to completion, then assert every key is reachable via point-GET (today keys whose new shard >= 64 would 404) and ListObjectVersions emits each key exactly once; capture the test failing against the skeleton, passing after
Versioned keys: every version of a key moves together; latest-version ordering preserved post-flip
make vet passes (Cassandra leg under -tags integration)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Cassandra implements optional meta.ReshardMigrator (MigrateReshardKey): relocates every version row of a key src-shard→dst-shard via INSERT … IF NOT EXISTS (MapScanCAS; never clobbers a newer concurrent target write) then deletes the source orphan — idempotent + crash-safe. New helpers scanAllVersionsAtShard + insertObjectRowAtShard (full-column copy, mirrors PutObject INSERT exactly, NO bucket_stats bump) + objectSelectCols const (scanObjectLimit1/scanObjectByVersion refactored onto it). internal/reshard/runJob rewritten: walks the in-flight union ListObjectVersions key-by-key, drives MigrateReshardKey per distinct key (dedup via lastMigrated) under a LastKey watermark with a forward-progress guard (inclusive marker + over-large-key), accumulates moved rows; RunOnce flips via LWT CompleteReshard ONLY after the walk drains (cleanup-before-flip). Memory/TiKV don't implement the interface → immediate-complete no-op (0 moved). Red/green proof: integration TestCassandraReshardWorkerMovesRows (64→128, 300 unversioned + one 3-version key; post-flip every key point-GETs, LIST emits each once, all 3 versions reachable in order — RED against the row-walking skeleton that 404s the diverging half). Worker unit tests use a recordingStore meta.ReshardMigrator fake (dedup-per-key + watermark resume stay meaningful + green on memory). strata-correctness-reviewer: no blocking issues, all 5 verifications pass. Narrow known race (concurrent specific-version DELETE vs copy window) named in code + ROADMAP P2. make vet + reshard/memory/s3api suites green locally CGO_ENABLED=0; cassandra leg integration/CI.

userStories.title: Reshard semantics on TiKV + memory (immediate-complete no-op)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want StartReshard/CompleteReshard to have defined, tested behaviour on every backend so the admin surface is backend-safe.
acceptanceCriteria: TiKV internal/meta/tikv/reshard.go::StartReshard today runs a REAL pessimistic-txn reshard flow (locks the bucket key, validates target > ShardCount, queues a job) - rework it to an immediate-complete no-op: StartReshard returns a job that RunOnce completes at once with zero rows moved (TiKV is range-scan, nothing to move); the old job-queueing path is removed/short-circuited

Memory backend internal/meta/memory/store.go:2898 StartReshard (already implemented) gets the same immediate-complete no-op semantics; shard-agnostic flat map keeps the key set intact before/during/after
meta.IsValidShardCount (power-of-two, n&(n-1)==0 at store.go:1429) still gates the target on all backends so an invalid target is rejected even where reshard is a no-op
Contract case asserts the no-op semantics on TiKV + memory: a reshard request leaves the full key set readable before, during, and after; a direct API/CLI caller gets success, not an error
docs/site/content/architecture/sharding.md updated: reshard applies to the Cassandra fan-out backend; TiKV needs no resharding (range scan)
make vet passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Immediate-complete no-op realized in worker runJob: short-circuits (no walk, no watermark) when backend has no meta.ReshardMigrator (memory/TiKV). StartReshard queueing kept intentionally — decision #4 requires a job for RunOnce to complete, and the shared caseOnlineReshard contract (green on all backends) asserts target-stamp + ErrReshardInProgress + flip. Red/green proven (6->0 ListObjectVersions calls).

userStories.title: Online-reshard admin endpoint (async) + concurrent-write smoke
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to trigger a reshard and have it complete correctly while clients keep writing, proving the transitional model under load.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing endpoint reworked not built fresh: adminBucketReshard (internal/s3api/admin.go:343) today drives the reshard synchronously inline (worker.RunOnce in the request goroutine) - wrong once US-003 moves real data (a 64->128 reshard of a large bucket would block the HTTP request for minutes/hours); split into a trigger (queue the job + return a job id immediately) + a separate progress read (job watermark / % complete / state)

Async drive: reshard is NOT a registered tick-worker today (absent from cmd/strata/workers/; there is no --workers=reshard path - do not assume one); either (a recommended) register a real leader-elected reshard background worker that picks up queued jobs and update the CLAUDE.md note, or (b) run RunOnce in a server-managed goroutine keyed off the job row
admin: audit stamp on the trigger (endpoint currently has no audit override - add it per the CLAUDE.md 'every new admin write' rule)
GetReshardJob (already on meta.Store) backs the progress read on all backends

Smoke harness scripts/smoke-reshard.sh + make smoke-reshard against the Cassandra profile: seed a bucket, kick a 64->128 reshard, drive concurrent PUT/GET/DELETE throughout, assert (a) no client sees a 5xx or spurious 404 during the job, (b) the key set is identical before and after, (c) post-flip point-GET hits the new layout
Crash-resume leg: kill the worker mid-job, restart, confirm it resumes from the watermark and still converges to a correct key set
make vet passes; smoke documented in the operator runbook
Tests pass

userStories.title: Reshard progress in the web console
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to start and watch a reshard from the console - mirroring the existing drain-progress UX - so the feature is not server-only.
acceptanceCriteria: Bucket detail surfaces shard count + a Reshard action (target = next power-of-two, typed-confirm) when the backend supports it; on TiKV the button is disabled with an explanatory tooltip 'range-scan backend needs no resharding' (API = no-op success, UI = disabled, both consistent)
A progress indicator reads the US-005 progress endpoint (state + % complete + ETA) reusing the DrainProgressBar-style component pattern
Playwright spec covers: trigger -> in-progress -> complete, plus the TiKV-disabled state
Typecheck/lint passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Direct test matrix for DeleteObjects (closes R1)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the multi-object delete handler directly tested so its batch/partial-failure/versioned semantics are pinned, not just exercised incidentally by the race workload.
acceptanceCriteria: Table-driven handler test over newHarness covering: quiet vs verbose mode, mixed success + NoSuchKey rows, the 1000-key cap (1001 -> error), malformed <Delete> body, and the <DeleteResult>/per-key <Error> wire shape
Versioned-bucket leg: unversioned-style delete creates a delete marker; explicit ?versionId removes a specific version; both reflected in the response rows
Tests fail against any deliberately-broken assertion (sanity), pass on main
ROADMAP R1 entry close-flipped with the commit sha
make vet passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Object-access policy UNION ACL semantics (closes R6)
userStories.description: As an S3 client, I want a bucket policy granting me s3:GetObject to actually grant access even when the object ACL is private - matching AWS, where policy and ACL are a union, not an intersection.
acceptanceCriteria: requireObjectAccess (internal/s3api/access.go:104) is changed from policy-INTERSECT-ACL to policy-UNION-ACL: an explicit policy Allow grants access regardless of the ACL gate (and vice-versa), while an explicit policy Deny still wins (AWS precedence: explicit deny > allow)
PublicAccessBlock interaction preserved: RestrictPublicBuckets / IgnorePublicAcls still suppress the respective public grant (QA-cycle US-005 PAB tests must not regress)

Red/green proof: a test where an anonymous/non-owner GET is allowed by bucket policy but denied by a private ACL now returns 200 (was 403); TestPAB_* and the existing policy/ACL gate matrices stay green

Fixture rework: existing TestPolicyGate_* pass today only because the in-memory harness owner-matches the anonymous identity - flip to a genuine non-owner anon principal exercising the union; the owner-full-control path and the explicit-Deny-wins precedence each get a direct case (security-sensitive widening - prove it does not grant beyond AWS)
ROADMAP R6 entry close-flipped with the commit sha
make vet passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Per-chunk CRC32C on the read path, fail-loud (closes R9)
userStories.description: As a data-integrity-conscious operator, I want a flipped byte in a plaintext (non-SSE) chunk to fail the read loudly instead of returning a corrupted 200, so silent at-rest corruption is detectable.
acceptanceCriteria: data.ChunkRef (internal/data/manifest.go:102, proto message ChunkRef internal/data/manifest.proto:54) carries a per-chunk CRC32C checksum, set on PutChunks, schema-additive: Go field + proto field 6 (fields 1-5 cluster/pool/namespace/oid/size are taken; 6 is next free) + JSON omitempty; legacy rows with no checksum decode to zero and skip verification (zero = absent, not CRC of zero bytes - guard the sentinel)
GetChunks verifies each chunk against its stored CRC32C and fails loud (data.ErrChecksumMismatch, surfaced as a 5xx / aborted stream - never a silent short or corrupted 200) on mismatch
Red/green proof: flip a byte in a stored plaintext chunk -> read fails loud (today it returns a corrupted 200); a clean chunk reads normally; SSE objects unaffected (AEAD already covers them)

Range-GET boundary handling: the CRC covers a whole 4 MiB chunk but a Range request reads first/last chunk partially - pick and implement one explicit behaviour: (a default) read the full boundary chunk to verify then slice, or (b) skip verification on the partial chunk and bump strata_chunk_crc_unverified_total{reason=range_boundary}; fully-covered interior chunks always verified; document + test it
Verification on by default with env opt-out STRATA_CHUNK_CRC_VERIFY (default on); hot-path cost measured/noted
ROADMAP R9 entry close-flipped with the commit sha
make vet passes (memory always-on; RADOS leg under ceph/integration if a backend hook is needed)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Cassandra ListObjects fan-out rebuilt for production
userStories.description: As an operator running the Cassandra backend at scale, I want the listing fan-out to be bounded and consistent so concurrent listings don't exhaust goroutines/connections and reads have a defined consistency guarantee - not the silent cluster default.
acceptanceCriteria: ListObjects/ListObjectVersions fan-out is bounded: today internal/meta/cassandra/store.go::ListObjects (~line 827) spawns shardCount goroutines per request with NO semaphore - at high per-bucket shard counts x concurrent list requests this explodes goroutines and the gocql connection pool; replace with a bounded worker pool (knob, sane default) so concurrency is capped regardless of shard count

Read consistency pinned explicitly (e.g. LOCAL_QUORUM) on the listing queries rather than relying on the cluster/session default - the listing read-consistency guarantee must not depend on an operator not having changed a Cassandra default
Memory budget bounded: the cursor/heap-merge holds at most boundedConcurrency x pageSize rows in flight, not shardCount x pageSize; documented
Pagination cookies still resume per-shard cursors correctly under the bounded pool (no page gap/dup when concurrency < shardCount)

Red/green proof: a test driving many concurrent ListObjects against a high-shard-count bucket asserts goroutine count stays bounded (no linear-in-shardCount blowup) and the listed key set is correct/stable; a consistency test asserts a write at LOCAL_QUORUM is visible to the very next list at LOCAL_QUORUM
TiKV unaffected (single ordered scan, no fan-out)
make vet passes (Cassandra leg under -tags integration)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Cassandra integration tests back on a CI gate (parity)
userStories.description: As a maintainer aiming for TiKV + Cassandra parity, I want the Cassandra meta.Store LWT/Paxos contract running on CI so Cassandra regressions are caught, not just found locally.
acceptanceCriteria: Cassandra contract round-trips (TestCassandraStoreContract/*, GCQueueShardFanOut, VersioningSuspendedReplaceNull, plus the new shard/reshard cases from US-001..US-005) run on a green required CI check - delivered this cycle as a dedicated job on a beefier runner because the A1b parity decision does not allow Cassandra to stay untested

Root-cause the per-PR-runner JVM/Paxos starvation (heap cap / readiness / per-test resource limits) so Cassandra can rejoin the per-PR gate; if the root-cause lands this cycle run it per-PR, else the dedicated job is required and the root-cause is a tracked owned ticket (not 'someday')
The job is green and its name is added to branch-protection required checks (documented as a manual post-merge step if admin token is needed, mirroring the QA-cycle coverage-gate step)
ROADMAP 'Cassandra integration tests gated off per-PR CI' entry close-flipped or updated with the new job reference
CI config validates locally; make vet unaffected

userStories.title: End-to-end pre-prod validation walkthrough
userStories.description: As the release owner, I want one e2e pass that exercises the whole hardened path on both backends so the GO/NO-GO decision rests on observed behaviour, not story-by-story claims.
acceptanceCriteria: A single documented walkthrough (script + runbook section) brings up both the TiKV-default and the Cassandra-profile labs and exercises: per-bucket non-default shard count, an online 64->128 reshard under concurrent writes (Cassandra), DeleteObjects batch + versioned, a policy-only anonymous GET (union semantics), and a plaintext chunk-corruption fail-loud
The console reshard UI (US-006) is driven in the same pass
Outcome captured in a short readiness note (pass/fail per leg) committed under tasks/
